frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre if she already said no, leave it yaar. But if you're not sure, just ask clearly whether she's seeing someone. If she's committed then obviously back off properly — no point creating disrespect with another guy involved.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats another man's prior claim as the decisive reason to stop pursuing her, rather than her own refusal being sufficient. It encodes the surface manifestation of asking if she is 'seeing someone' as a filter before fully accepting rejection, framed as respect.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
